amendments.text: For each commuter category airplane, the accelerate-stop distance must be determined as follows: [(a) The accelerate-stop distance is the sum of the distances necessary to-- (1) Accelerate the airplane from a standing start to VEF with all engines operating; (2) Accelerate the airplane from VEF to V1, assuming the critical engine fails at VEF; and (3) Come to a full stop from the point at which V1 is reached. (b) Means other than wheel brakes may be used to determine the accelerate-stop distances if that means--] (1) Is safe and reliable; (2) Is used so that consistent results can be expected under normal operating conditions; and (3) Is such that exceptional skill is not required to control the airplane.
